steps.title: Fit line k from the graph
steps.content: Use a Desmos table on the shown line $k$. The line passes through $(0,3)$ and $(4,0)$. The regression line has slope $-\frac{3}{4}$, so line $k$ has slope $-\frac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: For a line perpendicular to $k$, flip and negate the slope: $$m_j=-\frac{1}{-3/4}=\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Read off the answer
steps.content: The equation is $y=\frac{4}{3}x-8$. That matches choice A, so A is correct.
